mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months following rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering algorithmic risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive alpha over 60-day horizons, or returns are negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and generate negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The negative predictive relationship reverses during tightening periods, yielding positive 20-day returns in >60% of declining INDPRO observations, or regime interaction term is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and prompts expectations of tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months following CPI increases, or the monthly correlation between CPI growth and subsequent weekly returns exceeds +0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 300,000 additions in a single month signals an overheating labor market that forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of months where payroll additions surpass 300,000, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a shift in consumer spending toward durable goods, reducing near-term corporate earnings visibility and generating negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows no statistical significance in predicting forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which boosts corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of rising INDPRO observations during recovery regimes, or the regime-conditional predictive power is indistinguishable from zero.
